title: Okta Supply Chain Attack Compromises Authentication Tokens for 10,000+ SaaS Tenants
content: Okta has confirmed a sophisticated supply chain attack in which threat actors compromised a third-party support vendor and accessed customer authentication session tokens. Attackers used stolen tokens to bypass MFA and gain administrative access to downstream SaaS customer environments. MITRE ATT&CK T1199 (Trusted Relationship) and T1078 (Valid Accounts) were the primary techniques. All Okta-integrated SaaS platforms should rotate session tokens and audit admin actions from the past 30 days immediately.

title: Kubernetes Privilege Escalation via Misconfigured RBAC Policies — Active Exploitation
content: NVD has published CVE-2024-21626 (CVSS 8.6), a container escape vulnerability in runC affecting all Kubernetes distributions using the default container runtime. Attackers gain host-level access by exploiting misconfigured RBAC ClusterRole bindings combined with the runC flaw. Cloud-native organizations running unpatched Kubernetes < 1.28.5 are at critical risk. The attack chain maps to MITRE ATT&CK T1611 (Escape to Host).

title: Datadog Agent Vulnerability Allows Host Secret Extraction from Kubernetes Pods
content: A newly disclosed vulnerability in Datadog Agent versions 7.45.x allows a compromised containerised workload to extract the Datadog API key from the host agent socket, granting full observability-plane access. An attacker with pod exec access could pivot to extract secrets from other monitored services. CWE-522 (Insufficiently Protected Credentials) is the root weakness. Remediation: upgrade to Datadog Agent 7.46.0+.
